Title: Function deficiency of GhOMT1 causes anthocyanidins over-accumulation and diversifies fibre colours in cotton (Gossypium hirsutum)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Coker 312; HS2 mutant; NCC-derived F4 lines
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates anthocyanidins to form methylated anthocyanidins such as peonidin, petunidin, and malvidin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms methylated anthocyanidin products and influences anthocyanidin composition and total accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for anthocyanidin methylation; decisive for composition shift
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant pigmentation; fibre colour diversification; stress-related flavonoid functions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, stems, petals, bolls, testa, and developing fibres in hybrid lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed from At-subgenome copy in C312 leaves; Dt copy expressed at very low level
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript hardly detectable in HS2 leaves and low in derived coloured lines carrying HS2 allele
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard greenhouse/field growth; developing fibres at different DPA stages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA co-segregation analysis; qRT-PCR; overexpression; RNAi; complementation in HS2; HPLC-LC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or knockdown of GhOMT1 blocks methylated anthocyanidin formation, increases unmethylated anthocyanidins, causes whole-plant purple phenotype, and enables broader fibre colour diversification after crossing with naturally coloured cotton
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T-DNA insertion upstream of GhOMT1 caused drastic down-regulation of the At-subgenome GhOMT1 copy in HS2. 2. HS2 and GhOMT1-RNAi lines lacked or nearly lacked methylated anthocyanidins peonidin, petunidin, and malvidin, while unmethylated anthocyanidins accumulated. 3. GhOMT1 overexpression partially complemented HS2 and restored greener phenotype. 4. GhOMT1 deficiency was associated with up-regulation of flavonoid core-pathway genes and, after crossing with NCC lines, contributed to increased PA accumulation and novel fibre colours.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant effect on plant height or boll number in HS2; slight decrease in chlorophyll content
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable breeding target for creating novel naturally coloured cotton; can be combined with GhANR and GhLAR up-regulation or manipulation of anthocyanidin pathway genes to diversify fibre colours; purple vegetative phenotype can serve as codominant selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A homolog from Dt subgenome, GH_D01G2164, is naturally expressed at very low level and has a 65-bp deletion near the start codon in transcript; the phenotype is mainly associated with loss of the At-subgenome copy

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Pathway flux-directing reductase treated as regulator of PA accumulation in breeding context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: HS2 crosses with NCC varieties ZX1 and LX1-derived F4 lines
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes conversion of anthocyanidins into flavan-3-ols for PA formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation in developing fibres of HS2 × NCC progenies is associated with increased PA biosynthesis and darker/novel fibre colours
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key enzyme determining PA accumulation from anthocyanidin substrates in developing fibres
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fibre pigmentation in naturally coloured cotton
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low in leaves; significantly up-regulated in developing fibres of F4 progenies, especially from 9 DPA onward
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Crossing HS2 with naturally coloured cotton; fibre developmental stages 3–15 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in leaves and fibres across developmental stages; phenotypic analysis of fibre colour in progenies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhANR was not elevated in HS2 fibres, consistent with white fibres despite anthocyanidin accumulation. 2. GhANR was significantly up-regulated in developing fibres of HS2 × NCC progenies. 3. Increased GhANR expression correlated with increased PA biosynthesis and improved fibre colours.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to combine with GhOMT1 deficiency and other flavonoid genes to increase PA level and diversify cotton fibre colour
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although biochemically an enzyme, the paper emphasizes its flux-control role in PA accumulation in crosses

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Pathway flux-directing reductase treated as regulator of PA accumulation in breeding context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: HS2 crosses with NCC varieties ZX1 and LX1-derived F4 lines
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes conversion of leucoanthocyanidins into flavan-3-ols for PA formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation in developing fibres of HS2 × NCC progenies is associated with increased PA biosynthesis and novel/darker fibre colours
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key enzyme determining PA accumulation from leucoanthocyanidin substrates in developing fibres
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fibre pigmentation in naturally coloured cotton
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low in leaves; notably up-regulated at all examined fibre developmental stages in F4 progenies
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Crossing HS2 with naturally coloured cotton; fibre developmental stages 3–15 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in leaves and fibres across developmental stages; phenotypic analysis of fibre colour in progenies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhLAR was not enhanced in HS2 fibres, likely limiting PA production and leaving HS2 fibre white. 2. GhLAR was notably up-regulated in all stages of developing fibres in HS2 × NCC progenies. 3. Increased GhLAR expression correlated with elevated PA biosynthesis and improved fibre colours.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for raising PA levels and generating novel cotton fibre colours when combined with GhOMT1 deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although biochemically an enzyme, the paper emphasizes its pivotal control of PA flux in developing fibre

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Associated with GhOMT1 deficiency state in HS2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Putative transcriptional activation via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanidin biosynthetic genes through a putative MYB113-GL3-TTG1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation is associated with enhanced anthocyanidin accumulation in HS2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Proposed positive regulator contributing to elevated anthocyanidin biosynthesis in HS2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in HS2 according to RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB113 was up-regulated in HS2. 2. The paper proposes that MYB113 may activate anthocyanidin biosynthesis by forming a complex with GL3 and TTG1. 3. Its precise causal role in HS2 remains to be further demonstrated.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback-associated induction under blocked anthocyanidin methylation, but not directly demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential TF target to further enhance anthocyanidin and PA accumulation for fibre colour improvement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference is based on expression data and known functions in other systems; direct functional validation in this study was not performed
